methodology.formula: counted weight(op) = max(alpha_raw(op) - overhead_for_category, 0.0); known analytical zero-FLOP ops use weight 0.0
methodology.normalization: subtract per-category ufunc overhead; known zero-FLOP ops use weight 0.0
methodology.note: analytical_FLOPs from whest registry (FMA=1); perf_instructions are SIMD-width-weighted fp_arith_inst_retired counts; ufunc overhead subtracted per category; integer/bitwise ops use instructions counter; known zero-FLOP ops are injected from the curated registry

perf_vs_timing.note: scipy not available — correlation stats skipped

isnat.notes: Weight=1.0. datetime64 array overhead dominates measurement (noop=248 instr/elem, isnat=232). Actual NaT check is trivial.

angle.notes: Return angle of complex argument element-wise.

imag.notes: Return imaginary part of complex array.

real.notes: Return real part of complex array.

real_if_close.notes: Return real array if imaginary part is negligible.

iscomplex.notes: Test if element is complex element-wise.

isreal.notes: Test if element is real (imag == 0) element-wise.

iscomplexobj.notes: Return True if input is a complex type or array.

isrealobj.notes: Return True if x is a not complex type or array.

vdot.notes: Dot product with conjugation; cost = N (FMA=1).

vecdot.notes: Vector dot product along last axis.

outer.notes: Outer product of two vectors; cost = M*N.

tensordot.notes: Tensor dot product along specified axes.

kron.notes: Kronecker product; cost proportional to output size.

fft.fft.notes: 1-D complex FFT. Cost: 5*n*ceil(log2(n)) (Cooley-Tukey radix-2; Van Loan 1992 §1.4).

fft.ifft.notes: Inverse 1-D complex FFT. Cost: 5*n*ceil(log2(n)) (Cooley-Tukey radix-2; Van Loan 1992 §1.4).

fft.rfft.notes: 1-D real FFT. Cost: 5*(n//2)*ceil(log2(n)) (Cooley-Tukey radix-2; Van Loan 1992 §1.4).

fft.irfft.notes: Inverse 1-D real FFT. Cost: 5*(n//2)*ceil(log2(n)) (Cooley-Tukey radix-2; Van Loan 1992 §1.4).

fft.fft2.notes: 2-D complex FFT. Cost: 5*N*ceil(log2(N)), N=prod(s) (Cooley-Tukey radix-2; Van Loan 1992 §1.4).

fft.ifft2.notes: Inverse 2-D complex FFT. Cost: 5*N*ceil(log2(N)), N=prod(s) (Cooley-Tukey radix-2; Van Loan 1992 §1.4).

fft.rfft2.notes: 2-D real FFT. Cost: 5*(N//2)*ceil(log2(N)), N=prod(s) (Cooley-Tukey radix-2; Van Loan 1992 §1.4).

fft.irfft2.notes: Inverse 2-D real FFT. Cost: 5*(N//2)*ceil(log2(N)), N=prod(s) (Cooley-Tukey radix-2; Van Loan 1992 §1.4).

fft.fftn.notes: N-D complex FFT. Cost: 5*N*ceil(log2(N)), N=prod(s) (Cooley-Tukey radix-2; Van Loan 1992 §1.4).

fft.ifftn.notes: Inverse N-D complex FFT. Cost: 5*N*ceil(log2(N)), N=prod(s) (Cooley-Tukey radix-2; Van Loan 1992 §1.4).

fft.rfftn.notes: N-D real FFT. Cost: 5*(N//2)*ceil(log2(N)), N=prod(s) (Cooley-Tukey radix-2; Van Loan 1992 §1.4).

fft.irfftn.notes: Inverse N-D real FFT. Cost: 5*(N//2)*ceil(log2(N)), N=prod(s) (Cooley-Tukey radix-2; Van Loan 1992 §1.4).

fft.hfft.notes: FFT of Hermitian-symmetric signal. Cost: 5*n_out*ceil(log2(n_out)) (Cooley-Tukey radix-2; Van Loan 1992 §1.4).

fft.ihfft.notes: Inverse FFT of Hermitian signal. Cost: 5*n*ceil(log2(n)) (Cooley-Tukey radix-2; Van Loan 1992 §1.4).

linalg.matrix_norm.notes: Weight=1 (baked into cost function). Elementwise norms cost numel. SVD-based norms (ord=2, nuc) cost 4*m*n*min(m,n) — the 4x is baked in for SVD consistency.

linalg.matrix_power.notes: Matrix power. Cost: $(\lfloor\log_2 k\rfloor + \text{popcount}(k) - 1) \cdot n^3$ (exponentiation by squaring).

linalg.multi_dot.notes: Chain matmul. Cost: sum of optimal chain matmul costs (CLRS §15.2).

linalg.norm.notes: Weight=1 (baked into cost function). Elementwise norms cost numel. SVD-based norms (ord=2, nuc) cost 4*m*n*min(m,n) — the 4x is baked in for SVD consistency.

linalg.tensordot.notes: Delegates to `me.tensordot` which charges FLOPs based on contraction.

linalg.tensorinv.notes: Weight=4 (same as linalg.inv). Reshapes to 2D then calls inv. Cost n^3 in formula.

linalg.tensorsolve.notes: Weight=4 (same as linalg.solve). Reshapes to 2D then calls solve. Cost n^3 in formula.

linalg.trace.notes: Blacklisted per reviewer — datetime ops not in scope.

linalg.vector_norm.notes: Vector norm. Cost: numel (or 2*numel for general p-norm).

trace.notes: Re-benchmarked with np.ones setup to avoid random-gen overhead. Trace is a sum of diagonal — mode=ufunc_reduction.

abs.notes: Timing-based (fp_arith_inst_retired blind to this op). Ratio vs add=0.8690

negative.notes: Timing-based (fp_arith_inst_retired blind to this op). Ratio vs add=0.8447

positive.notes: Timing-based (fp_arith_inst_retired blind to this op). Ratio vs add=0.9146

ceil.notes: Timing-based (fp_arith_inst_retired blind to this op). Ratio vs add=0.8783

floor.notes: Timing-based (fp_arith_inst_retired blind to this op). Ratio vs add=0.8783

trunc.notes: Timing-based (fp_arith_inst_retired blind to this op). Ratio vs add=0.8777

rint.notes: Timing-based (fp_arith_inst_retired blind to this op). Ratio vs add=0.8722

sign.notes: Timing-based (fp_arith_inst_retired blind to this op). Ratio vs add=1.0057

signbit.notes: Timing-based (fp_arith_inst_retired blind to this op). Ratio vs add=0.3407

fabs.notes: Timing-based (fp_arith_inst_retired blind to this op). Ratio vs add=1.1184

logical_not.notes: Timing-based (fp_arith_inst_retired blind to this op). Ratio vs add=0.4378

frexp.notes: Decompose x into mantissa and exponent element-wise.

modf.notes: Return fractional and integral parts element-wise.

sinc.notes: Re-benchmarked on c6i.metal with correct input. α_raw=41.4251, overhead=0.3001, weight=41.1250

i0.notes: Re-benchmarked on c6i.metal with correct input. α_raw=111.6746, overhead=0.3001, weight=111.3745

spacing.notes: Return ULP spacing for each element.

nan_to_num.notes: Timing-based (fp_arith_inst_retired blind to this op). Ratio vs add=3.0865

isneginf.notes: Timing-based (fp_arith_inst_retired blind to this op). Ratio vs add=0.8331

isposinf.notes: Timing-based (fp_arith_inst_retired blind to this op). Ratio vs add=0.9379

fmod.notes: Timing-based (fp_arith_inst_retired blind to comparisons). Ratio vs add=5.5996

greater.notes: Timing-based (fp_arith_inst_retired blind to comparisons). Ratio vs add=0.5759

greater_equal.notes: Timing-based (fp_arith_inst_retired blind to comparisons). Ratio vs add=0.5734

less.notes: Timing-based (fp_arith_inst_retired blind to comparisons). Ratio vs add=0.5761

less_equal.notes: Timing-based (fp_arith_inst_retired blind to comparisons). Ratio vs add=0.5745

equal.notes: Timing-based (fp_arith_inst_retired blind to comparisons). Ratio vs add=0.5761

not_equal.notes: Timing-based (fp_arith_inst_retired blind to comparisons). Ratio vs add=0.5736

logical_and.notes: Timing-based (fp_arith_inst_retired blind to comparisons). Ratio vs add=0.8026

logical_or.notes: Timing-based (fp_arith_inst_retired blind to comparisons). Ratio vs add=0.7986

logical_xor.notes: Timing-based (fp_arith_inst_retired blind to comparisons). Ratio vs add=0.8007

copysign.notes: Timing-based (fp_arith_inst_retired blind to comparisons). Ratio vs add=1.1021

nextafter.notes: Timing-based weight (fp_arith_inst_retired blind to this op). Ratio vs add=5.7999

ldexp.notes: Timing-based (fp_arith_inst_retired blind to comparisons). Ratio vs add=3.3667

heaviside.notes: Timing-based (fp_arith_inst_retired blind to comparisons). Ratio vs add=1.3916

polyval.notes: Evaluate polynomial at given points. Cost: $m \cdot \text{deg}$ (Horner's method, FMA=1).

polyfit.notes: Least squares polynomial fit. Cost: 2 * m * (deg+1)^2 FLOPs.

polyadd.notes: Add two polynomials. Cost: max(n1, n2) FLOPs.

polysub.notes: Difference (subtraction) of two polynomials. Cost: max(n1, n2) FLOPs.

polymul.notes: Multiply polynomials. Cost: n1 * n2 FLOPs.

polydiv.notes: Divide one polynomial by another. Cost: n1 * n2 FLOPs.

roots.notes: Return roots of polynomial with given coefficients. Cost: $n^3$ (companion matrix eig, simplified).

random.choice.notes: Sampling; cost = numel(output) if replace, n*ceil(log2(n)) if not.

argmax.notes: Timing-based weight (fp_arith_inst_retired blind to this op). Ratio vs add=0.2320

argmin.notes: Timing-based weight (fp_arith_inst_retired blind to this op). Ratio vs add=0.2313

any.notes: Timing-based weight (fp_arith_inst_retired blind to this op). Ratio vs add=0.2513

all.notes: Timing-based weight (fp_arith_inst_retired blind to this op). Ratio vs add=0.2525

median.notes: Timing-based weight (fp_arith_inst_retired blind to this op). Ratio vs add=5.3855

nanmedian.notes: Timing-based weight (fp_arith_inst_retired blind to this op). Ratio vs add=5.7796

percentile.notes: Timing-based weight (fp_arith_inst_retired blind to this op). Ratio vs add=6.5693

nanpercentile.notes: Timing-based weight (fp_arith_inst_retired blind to this op). Ratio vs add=6.9821

quantile.notes: Timing-based weight (fp_arith_inst_retired blind to this op). Ratio vs add=6.5837

nanquantile.notes: Timing-based weight (fp_arith_inst_retired blind to this op). Ratio vs add=6.9870

count_nonzero.notes: Timing-based weight (fp_arith_inst_retired blind to this op). Ratio vs add=0.7773

nanargmax.notes: Timing-based weight (fp_arith_inst_retired blind to this op). Ratio vs add=1.4897

nanargmin.notes: Timing-based weight (fp_arith_inst_retired blind to this op). Ratio vs add=1.4777

sort.notes: Comparison sort; cost = n*ceil(log2(n)) per slice.

argsort.notes: Indirect sort; cost = n*ceil(log2(n)) per slice.

argpartition.notes: Indirect partition; cost = n per slice.

bartlett.notes: Bartlett window. Cost: n (one linear eval per sample).

blackman.notes: Blackman window. Cost: 3*n (three cosine terms per sample).

hamming.notes: Hamming window. Cost: n (one cosine per sample).

hanning.notes: Hanning window. Cost: n (one cosine per sample).

kaiser.notes: Kaiser window. Cost: 3*n (Bessel function eval per sample).

random.bytes.notes: EC2 timing = 0.86. Fast PRNG byte generation — cheaper than add.

isnan.notes: Timing-based (fp_arith_inst_retired blind to this op). Ratio vs add=0.3375

isinf.notes: Timing-based (fp_arith_inst_retired blind to this op). Ratio vs add=0.3493

isfinite.notes: Timing-based (fp_arith_inst_retired blind to this op). Ratio vs add=0.3454

matvec.notes: EC2 timing = 0.56. BLAS matrix-vector product — efficient.

vecmat.notes: EC2 timing = 0.61. BLAS vector-matrix product — efficient.

apply_along_axis.notes: EC2 timing = 1.95. Note: cost formula (result.size) underestimates — actual work is input.size.

apply_over_axes.notes: EC2 timing = 0.14. Efficient numpy reduction path.

array.notes: Timing ratio vs add = 0.9035. Benchmark: 10,000,000 elements.

block.notes: Formerly-free op. Timing ratio vs add = 0.0167.

bmat.notes: EC2 timing = 3.00. Block matrix assembly from nested list.

diag.notes: EC2 timing = 0.38 (1D->2D). Formula fixed to numel(output) for construction.

diagflat.notes: EC2 timing = 0.23. Formula fixed from len(v) to numel(output).

einsum_path.notes: Weight=1 by design. Path planning only — intentionally charges 1 FLOP for budget tracking.

fill_diagonal.notes: EC2 timing = 6.26 per diagonal element. Strided cache misses in large matrices.

fromiter.notes: EC2 timing = 20.38. Python iterator overhead per element.

put_along_axis.notes: Formerly-free op. Timing ratio vs add = 0.0310.

take_along_axis.notes: Formerly-free op. Timing ratio vs add = 0.4811.

unstack.notes: EC2 timing = 0.0007. Returns views — should be free.
